votes.reason: The Python code correctly translates the Coq specification, checking the length of the string and verifying if the last character is an alphabetical letter and, if the string has length > 1, whether the preceding character is a space.

The Python code accurately translates the Coq specification, checking the same conditions for the string length and correctly verifying if the last character is a letter and the preceding character is a space.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking the length of the string and verifying if the last character is a letter and, if applicable, preceded by a space.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. For empty strings, it checks res == False. For single-character strings, it checks if res equals whether that character is alphabetic. For longer strings, it checks if res equals whether the last character is alphabetic AND the second-to-last character is a space. The is_alpha function correctly checks ASCII ranges 65-90 (A-Z) and 97-122 (a-z). The logic matches the Coq spec exactly for all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. For empty strings, it checks res == False. For single-character strings, it checks if res equals is_alpha of that character. For longer strings, it checks if res equals (is_alpha(last_char) and prev_char == ' '). The is_alpha function correctly checks ASCII ranges 65-90 (A-Z) and 97-122 (a-z). The logic matches the Coq spec for all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it handles empty strings (returning res == False), single-character strings (checking if the character is alphabetic), and longer strings (checking if the last character is alphabetic AND the second-to-last character is a space). The is_alpha function correctly checks ASCII ranges 65-90 (A-Z) and 97-122 (a-z). The logic matches the Coq specification for all concrete inputs.

Matches the Coq spec on all cases: empty -> res=false; length 1 -> res equals ASCII A-Z/a-z test of the only char; length >=2 -> res equals (ASCII-letter last char) and (previous char is space). Character and space checks align with ASCII codes.

The Python code mirrors the Coq logic on ASCII strings: same letter check (A-Z, a-z), same length cases, and the two-char-or-more case requiring a space before the last character. Indexing and equality match Coq’s behavior, so results align on all concrete inputs in the Coq domain.

Python mirrors the Coq spec: it checks empty -> res False; length 1 -> res equals ASCII alpha test; length >=2 -> res equals (last char is A-Z/a-z and previous char is space). ord-based alpha check and char equality match Coq’s is_alpha and Ascii.eqb on ASCII strings.
